channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Israel Strikes Beirut Suburbs Despite Ceasefire
item.description: Israel attacked Beirut's southern suburbs days after a US-brokered ceasefire, risking renewed regional escalation as Iran warned strikes on Beirut could restart full-scale war across the Middle East.

item.title: Russian Drone Strikes Nuclear Fuel Facility Near Chernobyl
item.description: A Russian Shahed drone hit a nuclear fuel storage facility near Chernobyl, causing a fire and significant damage. Ukraine reported no radiation spike, but the IAEA called the attack deeply concerning.

item.title: US Approves $1.98 Billion Counter-Drone Defense Sale to Kuwait
item.description: The State Department approved a potential $1.98 billion arms sale to Kuwait for counter-UAV systems including Roadrunner-Munition and Anvil-Kinetic interceptors, strengthening Gulf defenses against drone threats.

item.title: Nigerian Army Rescues 360 Hostages from Boko Haram Stronghold
item.description: Nigerian forces freed 360 kidnapped victims from a Boko Haram enclave in the Mandara Mountains after weeks of intelligence-gathering and surveillance operations.

item.title: Xi Jinping Visits North Korea for First Time in Seven Years
item.description: China's leader travels to Pyongyang to reassert influence over Kim Jong Un as North Korea's nuclear arsenal expands and its alliance with Russia deepens, signaling Beijing's continued role as Pyongyang's key patron.

item.title: One killed, five wounded in central Israel terror shooting spree
item.description: A terrorist attack in communities bordering the West Bank north of Tel Aviv left one person dead and five wounded, with two in serious condition. One perpetrator was shot during the rampage.

item.title: Bridge linking Crimea to southern Ukraine damaged in drone strike
item.description: A drone strike damaged a bridge near the village of Chonhar connecting occupied Crimea with Russian-controlled parts of southern Ukraine, impacting a key logistics route for Russian forces.

item.title: Trump-Xi summit on Taiwan offered more than optics
item.description: The meeting between Chinese and US presidents laid the groundwork for more predictable China-US relations, including on the Taiwan issue, beyond just symbolic photo opportunities.

item.title: US Demands Iran Disclose Nuclear Sites and Uranium Stocks at IAEA
item.description: A US draft resolution before the UN nuclear watchdog board calls on Iran to provide precise information on its bombed nuclear sites and enriched uranium stocks, escalating diplomatic pressure amid ongoing ceasefire tensions.

item.title: Russian Drone Hit Nuclear Fuel Storage Facility Near Chernobyl
item.description: A Russian drone struck a nuclear fuel storage facility near Chernobyl, Ukraine reported, damaging the site but causing no radiation spike. The attack raises new nuclear safety concerns.

item.title: Peru Votes in Tight Presidential Runoff
item.description: Peruvians vote in a closely contested presidential runoff election testing Latin America's political shift amid economic challenges and corruption concerns.

item.title: Armenians Vote Under Russian Pressure
item.description: Armenians cast ballots in parliamentary elections as Russia exerts pressure to prevent the country from drifting toward the West amid ongoing regional tensions.

item.title: Armenia Arrests Six Pro-Russian Opposition Candidates
item.description: Armenian authorities detained six opposition candidates linked to Moscow one day before the parliamentary election, amid accusations of foreign interference.

item.title: US Eyes Iranian Assets for Gulf Ally Reconstruction
item.description: The United States is considering using frozen Iranian assets to fund reconstruction efforts by Gulf allies, sources said, as ceasefire negotiations with Tehran continue.

item.title: Russia Downs 339 Ukrainian Drones
item.description: Russia's defense ministry reported intercepting 339 Ukrainian drones in the latest wave of attacks, highlighting continued aerial warfare on multiple fronts.

item.title: Russia Opens Door to New Relationship with US
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin signaled openness to a new relationship with the United States even as the Ukraine war remains deadlocked, according to NBC News analysis.

item.title: Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Power Restored
item.description: The Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant restored power supply after an outage, continuing operations amid ongoing safety concerns about the facility in occupied Ukraine.

item.title: US Troops and Families Adjust to New Normal in Iran War
item.description: American military personnel and their families stationed in the Middle East adapt to ongoing threats and disruptions as the US-Iran conflict continues.

item.title: Nigeria's Military Rescues 360 Hostages in Borno
item.description: Nigerian military rescued 360 hostages from Boko Haram captivity in Borno state amid ongoing counterinsurgency operations in the northeast.

item.title: Greece Arrests Palestinian on Terrorism Charges Linked to Hamas
item.description: Greek authorities arrested a Palestinian citizen on terrorism charges related to Hamas, according to an AP News report, in a case with potential regional implications.

item.title: Hundreds of Thousands Line Madrid Streets for Pope Leo
item.description: Over one million people filled Madrid's streets for Pope Leo XIV's Corpus Christi Mass and procession, one of the largest papal gatherings in Spanish history.

item.title: China's Xi and North Korea's Kim Project Confidence Amid Challenges
item.description: Xi Jinping's visit to Pyongyang signals China's strategic focus on North Korea as regional security dynamics shift with Kim Jong Un's expanding nuclear program.

item.title: Pakistan's Naqvi Visits Iran with Special Letter for Supreme Leader
item.description: Pakistan's interior minister traveled to Tehran carrying a special letter for Iran's Supreme Leader as diplomatic efforts continue amid regional tensions.

item.title: Starmer, Merz, Macron, Zelenskyy Meet on Ukraine Support
item.description: British PM Keir Starmer, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, French President Emmanuel Macron, and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy held talks on continued support for Ukraine against Russian aggression.

item.title: US Resolution Text at IAEA Demands Iran Open Up on Sites, Uranium Stocks
item.description: A US draft resolution at the IAEA Board of Governors calls on Iran to provide full transparency on nuclear sites and uranium stockpiles, escalating international oversight demands.
